summary: Tested 5 new POS Ecuador features: Reports + exports, Suppliers + Purchase Orders + Receive Merchandise, Inventory CSV import + template, Barcode scanner buttons, Navigation. Backend: 24/24 pytest passed (100%). Frontend: All UI elements rendered, data-testids present, integration working. Found 1 minor pre-existing issue (Dialog missing aria-describedby - a11y warning only).

minor.issue: Performs an N+1 DB lookup per sale item (db.products.find_one inside loop). For large sale volumes this will be slow; recommend batch lookup or store categoria_nombre on the sale item.
Same path string with different methods – FastAPI handles ok but POST is declared before GET; consider explicit ordering for clarity.
Does NOT call validar_ruc_ecuatoriano even though it is imported. Any 13-char string is accepted as RUC. Frontend also only restricts to digits/length. Recommend validating RUC server-side.

No duplicate-codigo_interno check. Re-importing the same CSV creates duplicate products (only random IMP-xxxx fallback prevents collision when codigo is blank). Recommend upsert by codigo_interno per business_id.
Default IVA fallback is 15% (line 422). Ecuador current default is 15% so OK, but it silently applies when 'iva_porcentaje' missing – worth noting in docs.
Idempotency: Endpoint can be called multiple times on the same order and will keep adding stock each time. Recommend rejecting if estado=='recibida'.

issues: DialogContent missing Description/aria-describedby – accessibility warning in console (Radix UI).
Date pickers use browser-native <input type=date> instead of shadcn calendar – inconsistent with rest of UI.

action_items: Server-side validate RUC in POST /api/suppliers (call validar_ruc_ecuatoriano)
Avoid N+1 in /api/reports/sales-by-category by storing categoria_nombre on sale items or batching
Handle duplicate codigo_interno during CSV import (upsert or skip-with-error)
Add aria-describedby to DialogContent components to silence a11y warnings
(carry-over from iteration_1) PDF invoice 500, CORS wildcard+credentials, soft-delete inconsistency
critical_code_review_comments: import-csv parsing logic: 'get_val' lambda captures col_indices via closure; for CSV branch uses 'col' (alias string) but for XLSX uses index. Works but tricky – worth extracting to helper.
Suppliers create_supplier imports validar_ruc_ecuatoriano but never calls it.
receive-merchandise lacks idempotency / partial-receive bookkeeping (recibida marks whole order even if only some items received).
Inventory import-csv default iva_porcentaje=15 hardcoded – ok for Ecuador now but should come from business config.
Reports export PDF correctly uses bytes(pdf.output()) – fix learned from iteration_1 RCA was applied here, but invoice_routes.py PDF download still suspected broken (per prev iteration).
ConnectionStatus correctly hides when online & no pending (returns null at line 52).
test_credentials: owner@mitienda.com / Owner123! (tenant with business_id, branch + 1 product + 1 sale). admin@sistema.com / Admin123! (superadmin no business).
seed_data_creation: Test created suppliers (RUC 1792060346001 'TEST Proveedor Updated'), 1 purchase order (received, stock +5), and 2 products via CSV import (TEST_IMP_A_<unique>, TEST_IMP_B_<unique>) under owner@mitienda.com's business. Stock of first product permanently increased by 5 each test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py use module-scoped owner_session fixture (owner@mitienda.com). They mutate state: each run adds +5 to first product stock via receive-merchandise and creates 2 new TEST_IMP_* products. Duplicate-RUC test relies on previous test creating supplier in same session, so order matters. Frontend smoke-tested: navigation, all data-testids resolve, dialogs open, suppliers list populated with backend-created entries.
rca of the issue: No new blocking issues found. All 5 new features (Reports, Suppliers/Purchases, CSV Import, Barcode Scan buttons, Sidebar nav) are functional end-to-end. Minor improvements suggested above.
